notes: COVID-19 shock enters Australian and global financial markets. WHO characterized COVID-19 as a pandemic on Mar 11. Qantas announced that all regularly scheduled Qantas and Jetstar international flights from Australia would be suspended from end-March until at least end-May. RBA reports the ASX 200 was 35% below its Feb 20 peak by Mar 23. Use this as an equity-led demand-collapse break for energy exposure.
citation: WHO, Director-General's opening remarks on COVID-19, 11 Mar 2020 (https://www.who.int/news-room/speeches/item/who-director-general-s-opening-remarks-at-the-media-briefing-on-covid-19---11-march-2020); Qantas ASX and Media Release, Customer and employee impact of coronavirus related network cuts, 19 Mar 2020 (https://www.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20200319/pdf/44g63pr22trj3x.pdf); RBA Bulletin, Australian Securities Markets through the COVID-19 Pandemic (https://www.rba.gov.au/publications/bulletin/2022/mar/australian-securities-markets-through-the-covid-19-pandemic.html); EIA Today in Energy, Crude oil prices briefly traded below $0 in spring 2020 (https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=46336)

notes: WTI May 2020 futures settled at -$37.63 on Apr 20, the first negative settlement for the contract. The break reflects the COVID demand collapse, storage pressure at Cushing, and stress from the earlier Saudi-Russia price war. OPEC+ agreed to cut crude production by 9.7 mb/d from May 1, which provides a reasonable end point for the acute April oil-price regime.
citation: CFTC, Interim Staff Report on NYMEX WTI Crude Contract Trading on and around April 20, 2020 (https://www.cftc.gov/PressRoom/PressReleases/8315-20); EIA Today in Energy, Crude oil prices briefly traded below $0 in spring 2020 (https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=46336); OPEC Press Release, 12 Apr 2020 (https://www.opec.org/pr-detail/310-12-apr-2020.html); EIA Today in Energy, OPEC+ agreement to reduce production contributes to global oil market rebalancing (https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=45236)

notes: Russia launched its further invasion of Ukraine on Feb 24. EIA reports Brent crude prices moved above $100 per barrel after the invasion, the first time since 2014. Brent reached an intraday high of $139.13 on Mar 7 and settled at $127.98 on Mar 8 according to the UK House of Commons oil-prices briefing. Treat Mar 8 as the end of the acute shock window, while recognizing that the elevated energy-risk regime persisted through 2022.
citation: EIA Today in Energy, Crude oil prices rise above $100 per barrel after Russia's further invasion into Ukraine (https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=55020); UK House of Commons Library, Oil Prices briefing SN02106 (https://commonslibrary.parliament.uk/research-briefings/sn02106/)

notes: Hamas attacked Israel on Oct 7. When markets reopened on Oct 9, Brent settled 4.2% higher at $88.15 and WTI rose 4.3% to $86.38. Use this as the start of a Middle East geopolitical-risk window for oil prices, but do not treat it as a direct supply-disruption shock because early reporting noted no immediate direct oil-supply impact.
citation: Al Jazeera, Will global oil prices keep rising due to the Israel-Hamas war? (https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/10/10/will-global-oil-prices-keep-rising-due-to-the-israel-hamas-war); Reuters, Crude futures settle down on fewer worries of supply disruptions, 10 Oct 2023 (https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/oil-prices-dip-markets-debate-hit-middle-east-turmoil-2023-10-10/)

event: Iran first direct strike on Israel
notes: Iran launched more than 300 drones and missiles at Israel on Apr 13 in retaliation for the suspected Israeli strike on Iran's consulate in Damascus. Israel reported that 99% were intercepted. Israel carried out a limited strike near Isfahan on Apr 19. Brent briefly traded above $90 around the escalation, but the price spike was contained as the immediate exchange did not become a sustained oil-supply disruption.
citation: AP, Israel says Iran launched more than 300 drones and missiles, 99% of which were intercepted (https://apnews.com/article/33fcffde2d867380e98c89403776a8ac); Al Jazeera, Iran attacks Israel with over 300 drones, missiles: What you need to know (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/4/14/iran-attacks-israel-with-over-300-drones-missiles-what-you-need-to-know); CBS News, Stocks waver and oil prices rise after Israeli missile strike on Iran (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/israel-iran-missile-strike-stock-oil-prices/)

event: Iran second direct strike on Israel
notes: Iran fired a salvo of ballistic missiles at Israel on Oct 1 in retaliation for Israel's campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah. Reuters reports Brent settled at $73.56, up 2.6%, while WTI settled at $69.83, up 2.4%. The window runs to Israel's Oct 26 retaliation, which targeted military sites rather than oil or nuclear infrastructure, reducing the immediate oil-infrastructure risk premium.
citation: Reuters, Oil prices rise 3% after Iran launches missiles at Israel (https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-steady-prospect-more-supply-offsets-middle-east-conflict-worries-2024-10-01/); Reuters, Israeli military says missiles launched from Iran towards Israel (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israeli-military-says-missiles-launched-iran-towards-israel-2024-10-01/); Reuters, Iranian missile strike on Israel shows capability for greater scale and complexity (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iranian-missile-strike-israel-shows-capability-greater-scale-complexity-2024-10-02/); S&P Global Commodity Insights, Iran's missile attack on Israel spikes oil, gas, shipping market risks (https://www.spglobal.com/commodityinsights/en/market-insights/latest-news/natural-gas/100124-factbox-israel-hezbollah-escalation-highlights-risk-to-regional-oil-gas-shipping-markets)

notes: Trump announced reciprocal tariffs on Apr 2, including a 10% baseline tariff and additional country-specific rates. CEPR describes the announcement as the most extensive US tariff hike since Smoot-Hawley. AP and other market reporting document the sharp equity sell-off after the announcement and China's retaliation. On Apr 9, the White House paused most country-specific reciprocal tariffs for 90 days while keeping the 10% baseline and raising China-related duties to 125%. Treat the WTI/global-demand interpretation as market context, not a directly cited structural fact unless separate oil-price data are added.
citation: White House, Modifying Reciprocal Tariff Rates to Reflect Trading Partner Retaliation and Alignment, 9 Apr 2025 (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/04/modifying-reciprocal-tariff-rates-to-reflect-trading-partner-retaliation-and-alignment/); CEPR VoxEU, Tariffs, the dollar, and equities: High-frequency evidence from the Liberation Day announcement (https://cepr.org/voxeu/columns/tariffs-dollar-and-equities-high-frequency-evidence-liberation-day-announcement); CSIS, Liberation Day Tariffs Explained (https://www.csis.org/analysis/liberation-day-tariffs-explained); Reuters, What's in Trump's partial tariff pause? (https://www.reuters.com/world/us/whats-trumps-partial-tariff-pause-2025-04-09/); AP, Markets plunge with S&P 500 down 6% and Dow down 2,200 after China retaliates against Trump tariffs (https://apnews.com/article/d86db525c370e9da834e6dfb76e23b86)

notes: Israel launched a major operation against Iranian nuclear facilities, military sites, and regime infrastructure on Jun 13. The US struck Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan on Jun 22. Iran then fired missiles at the US Al-Udeid airbase in Qatar. A US-announced ceasefire took effect around Jun 24. Reuters reports oil prices fell sharply after the ceasefire announcement, consistent with a reduction in the geopolitical supply-risk premium.
citation: Britannica, 12-Day War (June 2025) (https://www.britannica.com/event/12-Day-War); UK House of Commons Library, Developments in Iran's nuclear programme and military sites, CBP-10284 (https://researchbriefings.files.parliament.uk/documents/CBP-10284/CBP-10284.pdf); Al Jazeera, US joins Israel in attacking Iran, strikes Fordow, Isfahan, Natanz (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/6/22/us-joins-israel-in-attacks-against-iran-strikes-key-nuclear-sites); Reuters, Oil tumbles to over one-week lows as Trump announces Israel-Iran ceasefire (https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-crude-oil-futures-fall-over-3-trump-announces-israel-iran-ceasefire-2025-06-23/)

event: 2026 Iran War / Strait of Hormuz closure
notes: US and Israeli forces launched strikes against Iran on Feb 28. Britannica reports that the initial wave targeted Iranian missiles, air defenses, military infrastructure, and leadership, and killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Iran's response and restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz created a major oil-transit shock. IEA describes the disruption as the largest in the history of the global oil market, with flows through Hormuz falling from around 20 mb/d before the war to a trickle. Reuters reports continuing high volatility in April and May, including Brent above $100 and a four-year high above $126 on Apr 30. End date reflects the latest data snapshot, not confirmed resolution.
citation: Britannica, 2026 Iran war (https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war); IEA, Oil Market Report - March 2026 (https://www.iea.org/reports/oil-market-report-march-2026); IEA, Sheltering From Oil Shocks (https://www.iea.org/reports/sheltering-from-oil-shocks); IEA, Strait of Hormuz (https://www.iea.org/about/oil-security-and-emergency-response/strait-of-hormuz); Reuters, Oil retreats after hitting four-year high on concern of US-Iran war escalation (https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-retreats-after-hitting-four-year-high-concern-us-iran-war-escalation-2026-04-30/); Reuters, Oil falls over 2% as Trump says he holds off scheduled attack on Iran (https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-crude-futures-fall-over-2-trump-says-he-holds-off-scheduled-attack-iran-2026-05-18/)
